problem_11.natural_language: If a linear endomorphism is nilpotent, then its trace is zero.
problem_11.lean:
theorem trace_nilpotent_eq_zero
    {K V : Type*} [Field K] [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V] [FiniteDimensional K V]
    (f : Module.End K V) (hf : IsNilpotent f) :
    LinearMap.trace K V f = 0
problem_11.error: /tmp/kb-compile-3xlscajh/Main.lean:18:8: error: No goals to be solved
problem_11.natural_language_proof:
Proof: choose `n` with `f ^ n = 0`. Consider the chain of subspaces
`0 ⊆ ker f ⊆ ker (f^2) ⊆ ... ⊆ ker (f^n) = ⊤`. Pick a basis adapted to this flag (extend a basis of
`ker (f^i)` to one of `ker (f^(i+1))` step by step). Then `f` sends `ker (f^i)` into `ker (f^(i-1))`,
so in this basis the matrix of `f` is strictly upper triangular; in particular all diagonal entries
are `0`. Hence its trace is `0`, and since trace does not depend on the chosen basis, `trace f = 0`.
problem_11.lean_proof:
by
  have h : IsNilpotent (LinearMap.trace K V f) := LinearMap.isNilpotent_trace_of_isNilpotent hf
  have : IsReduced K := by
    constructor
    · intro x hx
      rcases hx with ⟨n, hn⟩
      by_cases hx0 : x = 0
      · exact hx0
      · have : x^n ≠ 0 := by apply pow_ne_zero _ hx0
        simp_all
        contradiction
  apply (this.eq_zero) (LinearMap.trace K V f) h
